29.reason: [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right order once. This extra traversal prints all nodes in-order, which is not required and causes incorrect output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right order once. This extra traversal prints all nodes in-order, which is not required and causes incorrect output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right (in-order) traversal, but this extra function call prints all nodes in in-order, causing incorrect output. Also, the height calculation is flawed due to misuse of ++h.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right (in-order) traversal, but this extra function call prints all nodes in in-order, causing incorrect extra output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right order once. This extra call to perorder1 prints all nodes in inorder traversal, which is not part of the expected output and causes incorrect output format.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The problem only requires printing leaf nodes in left-to-right order once. This extra traversal prints all nodes in-order, which is incorrect and causes extra output.]

42.reason: [Using pre-increment (++h) modifies h for the current call, which affects the right subtree height calculation if both subtrees are processed. Height should be passed as h+1 without modifying h.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using pre-increment (++h) modifies h for the current call, which affects the right subtree height calculation if both subtrees are processed. Height should be passed as h+1 without modifying h.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using ++h modifies h and passes incremented value, which affects the right subtree call if it occurs; also, height should be passed as h+1 without modifying h.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using pre-increment (++h) modifies h and passes the incremented value, but the same h is reused in the next condition; also, height should be passed as h+1 without modifying h to avoid double increment when both left and right are evaluated.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using ++h modifies h for the right subtree call as well because h is passed by value but the increment happens before the right branch is processed. This leads to incorrect height calculation. The height should be h+1 without modifying h.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using ++h modifies h for the right subtree call if it were to happen; but more critically, passing ++h changes the height incorrectly because the same incremented h is used in recursive calls. Height should be passed as h+1 without modifying h.]

45.reason: [Same issue: ++h mutates h, leading to incorrect height values when both left and right recursive calls use the same incremented h. Should pass h+1 instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: ++h mutates h, leading to incorrect height values when both left and right recursive calls use the same incremented h. Should pass h+1 instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: ++h uses already incremented h from left branch or modifies original h; should use h+1 instead to correctly compute height.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: ++h uses already incremented h from the previous condition if it was executed, leading to incorrect height calculation. Should pass h+1 instead of using ++h.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue as above: using ++h after it may have already been incremented in the left branch leads to wrong height values. Also, since h is passed by value, using pre-increment here causes inconsistent height tracking.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: using ++h after possibly incrementing it in the left branch leads to incorrect height calculation. Also, if both left and right were called (which they aren't in a single path), this would be problematic. But even in isolation, reusing ++h on the same variable across conditions is error-prone. The correct approach is to pass h+1 directly.]
